meta.phase-name: AI Integration Architecture and Schema Authoring Plan

phase-plan.purpose: Define a concrete integration plan for Assistant UI + Codex CLI/app server, account-linked ChatGPT subscription reuse assumptions, and user-editable content model schema workflows that can ship in incremental slices. Includes the Product Owner agent (human stand-in for planning; all agents talk to it via MCP/Codex) and questions-per-phase workflow (phase questions drive DECISIONS and REQUIREMENTS updates).
phase-plan.scope: Planning-only phase slice: architecture, interfaces, dependency map, guardrails, and rollout sequencing. In scope: Product Owner agent role and MCP/Codex channel so it can answer questions and manage planning; alignment with questions-per-phase (see DECISIONS.xml PRODUCT-OWNER-AGENT, QUESTIONS-PER-PHASE). Out of scope for 50-01: production auth code, billing implementation, or full schema editor implementation.

file.description: Add Phase 50 roadmap entry and status for AI integration planning.
Track task ownership and status for 50-01 planning execution.
Register active agent id and current phase/plan pointers.
Record integration baseline decisions and references-source gap policy.
Primary planning artifact for AI integration architecture and rollout slicing.

questions.question: How does the Product Owner agent get exposed so Codex CLI / app server agents can talk to it? (MCP server tool, Codex SDK agent, or both?)
Where do phase questions live (inline in PLAN, or separate file) and how does the PO or CLI update DECISIONS/REQUIREMENTS from answers?

task.description: Capture current loop state and register unique agent id.
Define Phase 50 roadmap/task/state scaffolding with dependencies and references.
Draft implementation workstream breakdown for auth/subscription, assistant integration, schema editing surface, and observability.
Align Product Owner agent with Phase 50: define PO role in MCP/Codex stack, reuse planning-mcp or app server agents so one agent can act as PO (answer questions, manage planning).
Align questions-per-phase: ensure phase PLANs include questions; document how answers update DECISIONS and REQUIREMENTS (template and workflow).

decision.summary: Use Assistant UI and Codex app server as baseline integration stack; treat subscription/account boundaries as server-validated concerns.
decision.impact: Directs implementation toward explicit auth/session design before feature coding.

decision.summary: Use REQUIREMENTS.xml as the PRD/GRD direction source.
decision.impact: Keeps phase planning unblocked while preserving mandatory references in plan docs.
